items.title: OpenAI proposed donating 5% of its equity to a US sovereign wealth fund
items.author: Russell Brandom
items.content: <article>OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reportedly proposed giving 5% of the company’s equity to a U.S. sovereign wealth fund, reviving discussions about letting the public share in the financial gains from the AI boom. </article>

items.title: Popular TV-tracking app TV Time is shutting down as company focuses on AI
items.author: Sarah Perez
items.content: <article>TV Time, the popular TV tracking app, is shutting down on July 15 as parent company Whip Media pivots toward enterprise AI products.</article>

items.title: Lucid Motors’ CFO is out as its new CEO continues leadership shakeup
items.author: Sean O'Kane
items.content: <article>The company announced a new slate of executive hires meant to help turn things around, as Gravity SUV sales are not taking off as expected.</article>

items.title: US government says it got hacked — again
items.author: Zack Whittaker
items.content: <article>A top Democrat on the Senate's Intelligence Committee warned that the information accessed on a Homeland Security intelligence-sharing network may risk national security.</article>

items.title: Microsoft launches its own AI deployment company with $2.5 billion commitment
items.author: Russell Brandom
items.content: <article>Microsoft follows Amazon, OpenAI, and Anthropic with its new AI deployment group.</article>

items.title: Tesla saw a massive sales jump in the second quarter
items.author: Sean O'Kane
items.content: <article>The company delivered more than 480,000 EVs globally, seemingly thanks to geographic expansion and cheaper versions of the Model 3, Model Y, and Cybertruck.</article>

items.title: Rivian raises EV sales forecast as Q2 production ramps up
items.author: Sean O'Kane
items.content: <article>The company now expects to ship a few thousand more vehicles by the end of 2026 than it previously expected, after launching its R2 SUV last month.</article>

items.title: Yep, we’re using OpenClaw to date now
items.author: Amanda Silberling
items.content: <article>Ben Guez has "a bunch of potential international wives in [his] DMs," thanks to an automated script he set up using OpenClaw, Claude code, and Instagram trials.</article>

items.title: Indian tech tycoon bets $30M of his own money to build AI alternative to Microsoft Office
items.author: Jagmeet Singh
items.content: <article>Neo is Bhavin Turakhia’s fifth venture and his latest involving enterprise software. This time he's taking on Microsoft Office and Google Apps with AI.</article>

items.title: Apple is reportedly planning new iPad Pro and MacBook Pro releases early next year
items.author: Lucas Ropek
items.content: <article>Apple is readying several new iPad Pro tablets and a budget-friendly MacBook Pro, reports suggest.</article>

items.title: Bending Spoons defies SaaS slump, surges 40% on first day of trading
items.author: Marina Temkin
items.content: <article>The company has grown rapidly by acquiring and revamping last-generation tech brands like AOL, Eventbrite, Evernote, Meetup, and Vimeo.</article>

items.title: After $18B IPO, Bending Spoons founder says success comes from minimizing luck
items.author: Anna Heim
items.content: <article>The co-founders of Bending Spoons, the Italian company quietly buying beloved, ailing Internet brands, learned big lessons from their own startup's failure.</article>

items.title: WhatsApp usernames are already raising impersonation red flags
items.author: Jagmeet Singh
items.content: <article>Meta says usernames improve privacy, but critics question whether its safeguards can prevent impersonation.</article>

items.title: Apple’s Hide My Email feature has a bug that’s been exposing real email addresses, researcher claims
items.author: Lucas Ropek
items.content: <article>Research appears to reveal a bug that could render the feature effectively useless. </article>

items.title: SpaceX has an AI device prototype, and it sure sounds phone-ish
items.author: Rebecca Bellan
items.content: <article>SpaceX reportedly showed investors a "handset-like" AI device before going public. It could be another signal SpaceX wants to expand into wireless.</article>

items.title: Ashton Kutcher leaving Sound Ventures to launch new VC firm with Morgan Beller
items.author: Marina Temkin
items.content: <article>Sound built its reputation on concentrated, high-conviction bets in category-leading AI labs, while Kutcher's new fund appears to be chasing the layer underneath those companies — the infrastructure and energy that power them.</article>

items.title: Neocloud Together AI raises $800M, leaps to $8.3B valuation
items.author: Julie Bort
items.content: <article>The AI neocloud provider, which specializes in hosting open source models, last raised at a $3.3 billion valuation in early 2025.</article>

items.title: Lime begins life as a public company after years of uncertainty
items.author: Sean O'Kane
items.content: <article>The nine-year-old scooter and bike-share company has said it needs the funds to help pay down around $1 billion in liabilities. </article>

items.title: Cloudflare’s new policy pushes AI companies to pay for publishers’ content
items.author: Sarah Perez
items.content: <article>Cloudflare is giving AI companies until September 15 to separate web crawlers used for search from those used for AI training and agents, or risk being blocked by default on many publisher sites.</article>

items.title: Even Honda is pivoting to data centers
items.author: Tim De Chant
items.content: <article>Honda wants in on the lucrative energy storage market. This week it began producing batteries destined for data centers, not driveways.</article>
